title: Wellington in July
meta_desc: Plan a fun family trip to Wellington in July with kids, best activities, budget $200-380/day, top attractions like Te Papa and Wellington Cable Car
intro: Wellington is a fantastic destination for families, offering a wide range of activities and attractions to suit all ages. July is a great time to visit, with the winter school holidays offering plenty of opportunities for indoor and outdoor fun. With a daily budget of $200-380 for a family of four, you can enjoy the best of what Wellington has to offer.

sections.heading: Weather in July
sections.body:
Wellington can be quite chilly in July, with average high temperatures ranging from 12-13°C. It's also the windiest month of the year, so make sure to pack warm clothing, including hats, gloves, and scarves. However, the cooler weather makes it an ideal time to explore the city's many indoor attractions, such as Te Papa Museum and the Carter Observatory.

Rainfall is moderate in July, with an average of 12 rainy days throughout the month. But don't let that deter you - there are plenty of indoor activities to enjoy, and the rain can make the city's scenery look fresh and vibrant.

sections.heading: What's On in July
sections.body:
Wellington has a lively events calendar, and July is no exception. The city hosts a range of festivals and events, including the Wellington Winter Festival, which features live music, food, and craft beer. The festival usually takes place in the first week of July and is free to attend.

The Te Papa Museum also offers a range of exhibitions and activities during July, including the annual 'Bug Appétit' exhibition, which is perfect for kids. The exhibition is included in the museum's free admission and is suitable for children of all ages.
list: Wellington Winter Festival (first week of July, free admission)
Bug Appétit exhibition at Te Papa Museum (free admission, suitable for all ages)

sections.heading: Best Activities for July
sections.body:
Some of the best activities to enjoy in Wellington during July include visiting the Te Papa Museum, taking a ride on the Wellington Cable Car, and exploring the Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary. These attractions offer a great combination of indoor and outdoor fun, and are suitable for children of all ages.

The Carter Observatory is another great option, offering stunning views of the city and the opportunity to learn about the night sky. Admission to the observatory costs $15 per adult and $8 per child, and is free for children under 5 years old.
list: Wellington Cable Car (return tickets from $9 per adult, $4.50 per child)
Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary (admission from $29 per adult, $15 per child)

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Booking Ahead
sections.body:
July is a popular time to visit Wellington, especially during the school holidays. It's a good idea to book ahead for popular attractions like the Wellington Cable Car and the Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary, as they can get busy.

The Te Papa Museum is usually less crowded during the week, but can get busy on weekends and during school holidays. Admission to the museum is free, but some exhibitions may require a ticket, which can be booked online in advance.
list: Book ahead for popular attractions like the Wellington Cable Car and Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary
Te Papa Museum can get busy on weekends and during school holidays

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body: Here are some practical tips for families visiting Wellington in July:
list: Pack warm clothing, including hats, gloves, and scarves
Bring a waterproof jacket or umbrella to prepare for rain
Take advantage of the city's many indoor attractions, such as museums and galleries
Book ahead for popular attractions to avoid crowds
Consider purchasing a Wellington Cable Car return ticket, which includes admission to the Wellington Botanic Garden
